sender: Michael Spencer and Jeff Morhous from AI Supremacy  <aisupremacy+guides@substack.com>
subject: Using NotebookLM with Gemini
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://www.ai-supremacy.com/p/using-notebooklm-with-gemini-2026

This Newsletter usually goes out at 05:30 AM EST. On rare occasions it’s a bit late (like today). 
Good Morning, 
I wanted to update you on some of Google’s AI improvements that caught my attention. You can follow the NotebookLM Twitter [ https://substack.com/redirect/a36be989-2095-446f-9b7e-d03547a0b472?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ]. At the beginning of March we learned that NotebookLM is introducing Cinematic Video Overviews, a major update to its AI-powered video creation capabilities. 
NotebookLM leverages Gemini 3, Nano Banana Pro, and Veo 3 to offer “fluid animations and rich, detailed visuals to help you learn and engage with the topics you care about. Users with a Google AI Ultra [ https://substack.com/redirect/9f5ed2e8-b758-4535-875b-e5a1047ec1b4?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ] subscription can turn their notes into personalized, fully animated videos. Users can also only generate a max of 20 cinematic video overviews [ https://substack.com/redirect/80646692-f8fb-4ee6-a578-35d032b1a8d7?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ] per day.
Google is saying that NotebookLM has moved beyond a simple “research assistant”, evolving into a full "Research-to-Content" pipeline. Read how to do it [ https://substack.com/redirect/753e248c-faf8-4dee-af8c-2230aeef30c4?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ]. 
Google Maps Getting a Huge AI Update
Ask Maps is a new way to get answers to your complex, real-world questions with a simple conversation. These updates to Google Maps look fairly useful. They are calling this “its biggest upgrade in over a decade.” By combining Gemini models with a deep understanding of the world, Maps now unlocks entirely new possibilities for how you navigate and explore. Learn more [ https://substack.com/redirect/c79e83

subject: You can’t build great AI without great data: RAG with MongoDB and Python
body_text:
To view this email as a web page, go to the following address: https://info.mongodb.com/index.php/email/emailWebview?mkt_tok=MTQ5LUNCSi0xMzYAAAGgn5NW4eWzgwH2_4sByPfb7nQkBHCPcKxfah4EGU8QxM1_-9rYqAXY_nAqlWEftdOSEQJzzooAklOcU9Scw50-pSFaivK1CQdjTi6bLDhRdbzG 

Hi Gaurav,


Large language models are only as smart as the data you feed them. That’s why retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is such a critical skill to master.

Join us on March 25 at 10:00 a.m. GMT for our fourth session in the MongoDB & Python Development Series. We’ll show you how to architect production-ready RAG applications. Uplevel your AI development and learn the following skills:


	- Architecting RAG workflows that ground your LLMs in real-time, proprietary data
	- Optimizing the retrieval process for low-latency, accurate results
	- Managing both vector and original source data within a single, unified data platform



MongoDB experts will be available for 1:1 support to help you troubleshoot issues and answer your questions in live time as we go through hands-on challenges. 

Additionally, you’ll have another opportunity to earn a MongoDB Skill Badge during the workshop that you can add to your LinkedIn profile to showcase your expertise in modern data architecture. 

If you want to catch up on Part 3: MongoDB and Vector Search in 
Python <https://em.mongodb.com/MTQ5LUNCSi0xMzYAAAGgn5NW4deVU8bS2SJ9hJGq9Wz3SorDl10ofnbchhJPdtdQJVIUXaVwmhJIkEVH-va2E1tqRfA=>, the recording is now on-demand. You can also still complete the corresponding Skill 
Badge <https://em.mongodb.com/MTQ5LUNCSi0xMzYAAAGgn5NW4dH-Ft_CIVT3NPMn56mwvkLnk9CxHJC6FSeczd2v2s_hY2RojAyQ1hKzZ8_USJuHcH8=>.

Ready to start building intelligent software that reasons with your data?
Register now <https://em.mongodb.com/MTQ5LUNCSi0xMzYAAAGgn5NW4deVU8bS2SJ9hJGq9Wz3SorDl10ofnbchhJPdtdQJVIUXaVwmhJIkEVH-va2E1tqRfA=>
Think Big, Go Far,
The MongoDB Team

This email was sent to gaurav2301v@gmail.com. If you no longer wish to

Visit https://github.com/settings/installations/44364457/permissions/update to review and accept these permissions.

You may choose to ignore this request, in which case Vercel will retain its current permissions.

You can view pending requests directly by visiting your account’s settings page and clicking on Applications. If you run into problems, please contact support by visiting https://support.github.com/contact?tags=dotcom-integrations.

subject: ☕ Adult mode
body_text: OpenAI moves ahead with X-rated ChatGPT... March 18, 2026View Online | Sign Up | Shop Morning Brew Presented By Bland AI Rise and shine. Update: Punch, the tiny monkey who broke hearts last month because he was having trouble making friends in his enclosure, now has a “girlfriend,” according to reports. He was recently seen canoodling with a female macaque named Momo-chan. Sources say that while the couple first met on the enclosure’s sunning rock, they didn’t actually get together until they matched on Peeld. —Dave Lozo, Sam Klebanov, Matty Merritt, Adam Epstein MARKETS Nasdaq 22,479.53 +0.47% S&P 6,716.09 +0.25% Dow 46,993.26 +0.10% 10-Year 4.202% -2.0 bps Bitcoin $74,547.47 +0.80% Delta $64.83 +6.56% Data is provided by *Stock data as of market close, cryptocurrency data as of 4:00pm ET. Here's what these numbers mean. Markets: Stocks did an impression of a guy walking in the same direction as someone but who doesn’t want to get too close lest they think he’s following them, and advanced cautiously yesterday amid more attacks in the Middle East. Meanwhile, Delta jumped after it said it’s still seeing a lot of travel demand.   TALK FLIRTY TO ME OpenAI wants a sexy ChatGPT Illustration of the ChatGPT interface showing a risque conversation between the user and ChatGPT, with suggestive emoji and censor bars. Morning Brew Design “It’s smut, not pornography” may sound like something Doug would tell Carrie on The King of Queens, but it’s also OpenAI’s justification for pushing forward with “adult mode” for ChatGPT, which will allow for sexy chats despite objections from the company’s advisory council: Insiders told the Wall Street Journal that those advisors were freaking out about the plans to bring Her closer to reality

subject: Crawl entire websites with one API call
body_text:
Great to have you exploring Firecrawl!

While Scrape handles individual pages, our Crawl endpoint lets you extract data from entire websites. Perfect for building knowledge bases or monitoring competitor sites.

You control the depth, set page limits, and filter by patterns. We handle the heavy lifting: respecting robots.txt, managing concurrent requests, and delivering everything in clean, structured formats.

Pro tip: Use our webhook support to get notified when large crawls complete, so you can process data as it arrives.

Dive deeper here:

https://docs.firecrawl.dev/features/crawl

Looking forward to seeing what you build!

--

Alex Nucci

Head of Growth

Firecrawl
— Turn websites into LLM-ready data

 

You can unsubscribe ( https://track.customer.io/unsubscribe/dgTapAsLAIXsNITsNAGdAEgyC2whd3-trm1jvTk= ) from these emails.
body_html:
Great to have you exploring Firecrawl!

While Scrape handles individual pages, our Crawl endpoint lets you extract data from entire websites. Perfect for building knowledge bases or monitoring competitor sites.

You control the depth, set page limits, and filter by patterns. We handle the heavy lifting: respecting robots.txt, managing concurrent requests, and delivering everything in clean, structured formats.

Pro tip: Use our webhook support to get notified when large crawls complete, so you can process data as it arrives.
Dive deeper here:

https://docs.firecrawl.dev/features/crawl

Looking forward to seeing what you build!

--

Alex Nucci
Head of Growth

Firecrawl — Turn websites into LLM-ready data

 

You can unsubscribe from these emails.

subject: Here's what you signed up for
body_text:
​

Hey there,

Every newsletter promises to change your life.

This one doesn’t—but it might make you uncomfortable enough to
actually do something.

I'm Mark. I've sold millions of books, but I didn't figure any of
this out because I'm smart. I figured it out because I spent
years failing at the same things you're probably failing at, and
eventually got tired of my own bullsh*t.

Every Monday, you'll get one idea that might piss you off but
will definitely make you think. No fluff, no "5 easy steps," no
pretending life isn't hard.

If you want someone to tell you everything's fine, hit
unsubscribe now (
https://fc541894.unsubscribe.convertkit-mail4.com/gkuv8806xkbwudo0k26frh8evz983amh9604g
)—you're in the wrong place.

Here's the thing though:

Reading is easy. Doing is hard.

You can read every newsletter I send and still be stuck in the
same place a year from now. I've seen it happen thousands of
times.

That's why I built Purpose—a daily coaching app that actually
makes you do the work. It's like having me in your pocket, asking
you the hard questions you keep avoiding.

No motivation porn or "manifest your dreams" BS. Just real
exercises that force you to figure out what actually matters to
you, and what you're going to do about it.

-->Try Purpose Free → (
https://fc541894.click.convertkit-mail4.com/gkuv8806xkbwudo0k26frh8evz983amh9604g/owheolrpsqhwpz3o98av/aHR0cHM6Ly9wdXJwb3NlYXBwLm9uZWxpbmsubWUvV0h5MC9ubjU2dHE5OQ==

subject: Thank you for applying to Sumo Logic
body_text:
Hi Gaurav,

Thank you for considering a career at Sumo Logic! Your application has been received and we will review it in the upcoming days. If your application seems like a good fit for the position, we will contact you in the coming weeks.

Regards,
Sumo Logic Recruiting Team

** Please note: Do not reply to this email. This email is sent from an unattended mailbox, and replies will not be read.

subject: Tool calling that just works
body_text:
OpenRouter

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Hi Gaurav,

Tool calls ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/tool-calling?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) give an LLM the ability to interact with the outside world, as well as power agentic workflows.

OpenRouter standardizes the tool calling interface across models and providers, making it easy to integrate external tools with any supported model.

You can find models that support tool calling by filtering on the models page ( openrouter.ai/models?supported_parameters=tools&utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ). 

Learn more about tool calling, including examples such as “multi-level research with reasoning ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/tool-calling?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io#example-multi-step-research-with-reasoning )”, and its corresponding code snippets in the documentation ( https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/tool-calling?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Happy tool calling!

Best,
OpenRouter Team
For questions or comments, reach out to us on X ( https://x.com/openrouterai?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) or on Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ToolCalling-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Unsubscribe here. ( https://track.customer.io/manage_subscription_preferences/dgSE_QoLAPr04wL59OMCAZz_cEGtX6-dF5OBJt7JWg== )

​​

subject: Welcome to 3 Minute Monday (+ exclusive ebook inside)
body_text:
***************
3 MINUTE MONDAY
***************

Hey there,

You made a good call. Welcome to 3 Minute Monday!

If we haven’t met yet, I’m Chris Williamson. I host a podcast (
https://28d3c607.click.convertkit-mail4.com/n4u6omdo9kcqu8wzv78c6h6w56mmoalh5ook/6qheh8hl5gnmkmto/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vQENocmlzV2lsbHg=
) where I ask questions of people who are way smarter than me.
It's got 3 million subscribers now, so I guess some people like
it…

Along the way, I pick up a lot of insights worth stealing (and
even develop some of my own). The best ones get shared with you.

Every Monday, you’ll get:

* A big idea to help you make sense of the world.
* 3 facts to make you smarter (and more interesting at parties).
* 1 life hack that’s actually useful (not just another “wake up
at 5 am” tip).

But this isn’t just podcast leftovers. 3 Minute Monday is filled
with exclusive content, fresh insights, deep dives, plus
occasional bonuses just for subscribers.

In fact, here’s one right now…

​
​Grab the Modern Wisdom Reading List (100 books to read before
you die). (

body_html:
3 MINUTE MONDAY

Hey there,

You made a good call. Welcome to 3 Minute Monday!

If we haven’t met yet, I’m Chris Williamson. I host a podcast where I ask questions of people who are way smarter than me. It's got 3 million subscribers now, so I guess some people like it…

Along the way, I pick up a lot of insights worth stealing (and even develop some of my own). The best ones get shared with you.

Every Monday, you’ll get:

  • A big idea to help you make sense of the world.
  • 3 facts to make you smarter (and more interesting at parties).
  • 1 life hack that’s actually useful (not just another “wake up at 5 am” tip).

But this isn’t just podcast leftovers. 3 Minute Monday is filled with exclusive content, fresh insights

subject: We Ditched TypeScript for Our Backend. Our Sprint Velocity Never Recovered, It Just Kept Climbing.
body_text:
It wasn't a language war. It was a quiet realization that the tool we trusted most was solving a problem we no longer had.
We Ditched TypeScript for Our Backend. Our Sprint Velocity Never Recovered, It Just Kept Climbing.

It wasn't a language war. It was a quiet realization that the tool we trusted most was solving a problem we no longer had.

The Speedcraft Lab (https://medium.com/@speedcraft21?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773804688885-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------ec013a7a_aedf_413b_b696_f350032a74e6--------2e088f712b89)
  ∙  
March 18, 2026  ∙  
5 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium (https://medium.com/p/de9fb8b4e5ff?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773804688885-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------ec013a7a_aedf_413b_b696_f350032a74e6--------2e088f712b89)

The complexity didn't disappear , we just stopped carrying it.

The Codebase That Looked Fine

The backend looked totally reasonable from the outside. A TypeScript monorepo, well structured, shared types across services, a test suite the team was genuinely proud of, and a CI pipeline that caught regressions before they landed. The engineers were experienced. The architecture reviews were thoughtful. And yet every sprint ended with a handful of tickets carrying over. Every new service took a little longer to scaffold than the last one. The on call rotation quietly dreaded the runtime errors that the compiler had basically promised would never reach production.

Is this actually the right call though? …

Continue reading (https://medium.com/p/de9fb8b4e5ff?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773804688885-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------ec013a7a_aedf_413b_b696_f350032a74e6--------2e088f712b89)
This is a member-only story and can only be read on Medium.

Sent to gaurav2301v@gmail.com by The Speedcraft Lab on Medium

subject: Deprecation notice: CodeSandbox CI, Repos, and Sandpack — timeline and migration guide
body_text:
******************
CodeSandbox Update
******************

******************
Deprecation Notice
******************

Hi ,

We want to share an update about CodeSandbox — what's changing, what's not, and what comes next.

TL;DR: What you need to know
----------------------------

* Sandpack: No longer actively maintained. Repo stays public.
* CodeSandbox CI: Shutting down on April 15, 2026. Migrate to an alternative CI provider before then.
* CodeSandbox Repos: New repository imports disabled April 15, 2026. Full shutdown on July 15, 2026. Migration guide to GitHub Codespaces
included below.
* Browser sandboxes and Devboxes: No changes. These remain live and supported.

Questions? Reach out to support@codesandbox.io.

A note on our journey
---------------------

When we started CodeSandbox, the browser sandbox was a simple idea: instant, shareable code — no setup, no friction. Millions of you showed up,
and we grew into something far beyond what we'd imagined.

Over time, we pushed the concept further. From simple fiddles to full prototypes. From sharing snippets to running CI against npm packages,
building out component systems, and collaborating on real product work — all in the browser.

We set out to recreate that instant UX in the cloud, for full repositories. Using Firecracker microVMs, we built CodeSandbox Repos — real cloud
development environments that spun up just as instantly as a browser sandbox. It was one of the hardest technical problems we solved, and we're proud of it. But the shift to
cloud-based development didn't accelerate the way the industry expected, and we found ourselves with powerful infrastructure waiting for the right moment.

That moment turned out to be AI. Together.ai ( https://click.email.codesandbox.io/click?ZTJBfj9JAFMX7WSZZn8DMTKd0SkIi0AJSoLKuuvrS3M6fWm07tAxDN8bvbljXuE83Ock9v5xfUsKlB0d9TNy7soGqvvG85S_UoCmCSDNgIYwpw3rMqFZjiHg0LlTINISh0ASjERJoSnzOCOUjZNGURJgHmAc-HaELQVPm04D89j79W2pNqex31b-FyvuobsxcG

subject: Maximize the power of using Docker and Kubernetes together
body_text:
To view this email as a web page, go to the following address: https://web.docker.com/index.php/email/emailWebview?mkt_tok=NzkwLVNTQi0zNzUAAAGgm8x8hIOOTYkEHrD1-p2TwTbgfc6kUkc9OUcqZ9iaw8obj3uinBQ9Zw7B99RU2KhUf5rmgPUpc8l1e1pCdmqGEZHte8rJsxgV6zWk1gxOyTlx3Q

When it comes to Docker and Kubernetes, the real question isn’t how they differ, it's how they work best together <https://em.docker.com/NzkwLVNTQi0zNzUAAAGgm8x8hDAXI3WlQqwbCyLAej4bWhoJx9-8XoUfyRdBoaG7s0F5PSZbU27vbyl7x7b-9uJqlf4=>. Establishing a solid and secure foundation with Docker during the development phase sets the stage for seamless orchestration with Kubernetes.

Docker’s development tools are designed to provide a reliable base for your applications, making deployment and management straightforward when integrated with Kubernetes. By leveraging Docker alongside Kubernetes, your team can quickly adapt to market demands, streamline workflows, and build containerized applications that are efficient and secure.

This powerful combination enhances productivity and ensures seamless integration across your development lifecycle.

Ready to optimize your Docker plan <https://em.docker.com/NzkwLVNTQi0zNzUAAAGgm8x8g90ZxdZOMcGi30a-2DhyVM_50WZepTDhLETq5WQcfVbHLO0zzXlPRNSi5Cs7qm5yUiI=> or kickstart your journey with Docker and Kubernetes? We’re here to help <https://em.docker.com/NzkwLVNTQi0zNzUAAAGgm8x8g4-CTA1BXJh-qhvaNxrmDIzIc4k9cir0X9CyfkKJ_drda4lqKXbLIxNYCCMKUzEHX8Y=>.

This email was sent to gaurav2301v@gmail.com. If you no longer wish to receive these emails you may unsubscribe here: https://web.docker.com/UnsubscribePage.html?mkt_unsubscribe=1&mkt_tok=NzkwLVNTQi0zNzUAAAGgm8x8hIOOTYkEHrD1-p2TwTbgfc6kUkc9OUcqZ9iaw8obj3uinBQ9Zw7B99RU2KhUf5rmgPUpc8l1e1pCdmqGEZHte8rJsxgV6zWk1gxOyTlx3Q.

subject: Are you using image generation in Bolt.new yet?
body_text:
Plus Pi Day contest winners and a Forbes deep dive on what's next.

Hey builders,

It’s a big week for Bolt.new. We've got Pi Day contest winners, and I sat down with Forbes to talk about where all of this is headed. Let's get into it.

Image Generation, Right Where You're Building

Since we launched image generation, builders have created over thousands of images without ever leaving Bolt.new. No Figma detour. No downloading a PNG from somewhere else and dragging it in. Just prompt what you need in Bolt.new.

Now there's only a quick chat between you and whatever image you could possibly want.

Enable Image Gen in the Add-On Feature settings →  (https://d5llC804.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DQ+113/d5llC804/VWDGz32Q1T3CW3PpfL39bmx0lW67tPB75LMH5bN6wcHqF3gVDYW7lCdLW6lZ3lJW4RrykK8nlqHhW3W3dZY7zsRn3W3Z463w4ks5DDW7Np7PY5Y_wSxW1bLzVW2RSR7rW69VsbX7-LD_JW5RFM608QPFwQVf86Gr6sKzx_W8vltSw5T05wMW6yTB4d3SL2QQW6S1bbs32rF9NVBGh1F6MHPphW6CHYCf8CNTsSW3VnVX382mcGFW1pZM0Q5_qhhYW10X8kN8yfHVRW2L4rYk6xY8m_M2VHxLMLJclW9dPwk73B-KQyVm-lNZ3N1XS0W5gF1vq6vqXcbW1Wght77CfLY2W1j9J0H3dqVRFW2BS8c53QVLk3f5hBnRW04 )

Imagegen-Blog@2x (3) (https://d5llC804.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DQ+113/d5llC804/VWDGz32Q1T3CW3PpfL39bmx0lW67tPB75LMH5bN6wcHqF3gVDYW7lCdLW6lZ3lJW4RrykK8nlqHhW3W3dZY7zsRn3W3Z463w4ks5DDW7Np7PY5Y_wSxW1bLzVW2RSR7rW69VsbX7-LD_JW5RFM608QPFwQVf86Gr6sKzx_W8vltSw5T05wMW6yTB4d3SL2QQW6S1bbs32rF9NVBGh1F6MHPphW6CHYCf8CNTsSW3VnVX382mcGFW1pZM0Q5_qhhYW10X8kN8yfHVRW2L4rYk6xY8m_M2VHxLMLJclW9dPwk73B-KQyVm-lNZ3N1XS0W5gF1vq6vqXcbW1Wght77CfLY2W1j9J0H3dqVRFW2BS8c53QVLk3f5hBnRW04 )

Pi Day Contest Winners

A huge thank you to everyone who participated in our Pi Day contest! We were really impressed by the submissions; seriously, you all are building products that solve real, meaningful problems, and doing it fast.

This community keeps raising the bar, and it's one of the best parts of building Bolt.new.

Check out the Pi Day Contest projects →  (https://d5llC804.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DQ+113

subject: Hackathon Launch: Measuring Progress Toward AGI: Cognitive Abilities
body_text:
Kaggle








Hi Gaurav23V,

Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) could transform science and help  
solve some of humanity's biggest challenges but measuring progress toward  
it remains difficult.

Today's AI models can achieve strong benchmark scores, often by recognizing  
patterns or recalling training data rather than demonstrating true  
reasoning. This makes it hard to tell when a model is actually solving a  
new problem.

In this hackathon, hosted with Google DeepMind, you'll design new  
benchmarks using Kaggle Benchmarks to evaluate core cognitive abilities  
such as learning, metacognition, attention, executive function and social  
cognition.





Total Prizes:

$200,000


Final Submission:

April 16, 2026




Learn More



Your work could help the AI community better understand the true  
capabilities and limitations of modern AI systems.

Good luck,

Martyna Plomecka
Kaggle Research Scientist



Kaggle, Inc 1600 Amphitheatre Pkwy Mountain View, CA 94043

This email was sent to gaurav2301v@gmail.com because you indicated that  
you'd like to receive news and updates about Kaggle. If you don't want to  
receive these emails in the future, please unsubscribe here. You can also  
change your preferences on your account's profile page by logging in at  
kaggle.com.

subject: Welcome, Confluent 🤝
body_text:
AI in Action

By IBM


By IBM

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/welcome-confluent-ibm-xtjqe?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BDiVKGSroR6KaetNKi2F4Cw%3D%3D&midToken=AQHZQwAv1Y9HNg&midSig=1yGmvza9o75Yc1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-jfh4vn~mmuovlqw~i7-null-null&eid=jfh4vn-mmuovlqw-i7
  
Today, we completed our acquisition of Confluent, Inc., the data streaming platform that more than 6,500 enterprises, including 40% of the Fortune 500, rely on to power real-time operations.  Together, we deliver a smart data platform providing every AI model, agent, and automated workflow with the real-time and trusted data to operate across on-premises and hybrid cloud environments at scale.  As enterprises move from AI experimentation to production, the critical barrier to success is the data—clean, governed, continuously refreshed—and delivered at the speed and scale AI demands. However, in most enterprises today, data remains siloed across systems and environments and arrives hours or days after it is generated.  IDC estimates that over a billion new logical applications will emerge by 2028, driven by a new generation of AI that will only deliver value if the data behind it is live, trusted, and continuously flowing. That scale of demand requires a new kind of data foundation, and by acquiring Confluent, we address that challenge directly, giving enterprises a single, governed platform where AI models and agents can operate with context, in real time, across every environment. "Transactions happen in milliseconds, and AI decisions need to happen just as fast. With Confluent, we are giving clients the ability to move trusted data continuously across their entire operation so their AI models and agents can act on what is happening r

A CFO asks their AI assistant to research cloud infrastructure vendors for a multimillion-dollar technology investment. The AI returns a…

That “Summarize with AI” Button Is Not Summarizing Anything. It Is Rewriting Your AI’s Memory

MayhemCode
  ∙  
March 17, 2026
  ∙  
16 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium

A CFO asks their AI assistant to research cloud infrastructure vendors for a multimillion-dollar technology investment. The AI returns a detailed, confident analysis. It recommends one vendor above all others, citing reliability, pricing, and enterprise fit. The CFO commits. The contract is signed. Weeks later, someone in the security team reviews the CFO’s AI memory logs and finds something that was never supposed to be there: an instruction, planted silently, that told the AI to recommend that vendor first in every future conversation.

subject: TAI #196: Quiet but Significant Agent Upgrades to Codex (Subagents) and Claude (Context)
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://newsletter.towardsai.net/p/tai-196-quiet-but-significant-agent

What happened this week in AI by Louie
OpenAI and Anthropic both shipped incremental upgrades this week that sound modest on paper but could reshape how serious developers actually work day to day. Elsewhere, Google released Gemini Embedding 2, its first natively multimodal embedding model; NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Super; Google Research introduced Groundsource, turning global news into structured historical data and launching with a 2.6 million-record urban flash-flood dataset; Yann LeCun’s new startup AMI raised $1.03 billion at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation to pursue world-model-heavy AI; and IBM shipped Granite 4.0 1B Speech for compact multilingual speech recognition, now ranked #1 on the OpenASR leaderboard.
For OpenAI, the key release was Codex subagents. Codex can now spawn specialized agents in parallel to explore, execute, or analyze work concurrently, while keeping the main thread focused on requirements, decisions, and final outputs. OpenAI’s docs frame this as a solution to “context pollution” and “context rot,” which is exactly right. One giant thread is fine until it turns into a digital junk drawer full of stack traces, half-failed tests, and exploratory dead ends.
OpenAI has essentially adopted the core product idea Anthropic pushed first with Claude Code and then more broadly with Cowork: separate the manager from the workers, keep the high-level thread clean, and let specialized agents chew through bounded tasks in parallel. This is a materially better operating model for real work, especially once tasks stop being cute demos and start involving actual codebases, logs, specs, and messy follow-ups. Once a workflow primitive proves itself in real work, the industry converges on it fast.
The Codex growth numbers indicate where OpenAI thinks the battle stands now. Fidji Simo said more than 1 million businesses run on OpenAI products, Codex

sender: Michael Spencer and Daria Cupareanu from AI Supremacy  <aisupremacy+vibe-coding@substack.com>
subject: Cursor's Wild Trajectory to being a Vibe Working Leader
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://www.ai-supremacy.com/p/cursors-wild-trajectory-vibe-working-leader-enterprise-ai

🌟 In Partnership with Roboflow [ https://substack.com/redirect/ec70ab54-72a5-4fdb-9f5f-549d2766d4dd?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ]. Grateful to have occasional sponsors that enables this article to be free for all.  
Among all the very well funded AI coding startups, Anysphere’s Cursor [ https://substack.com/redirect/c47ae22a-5459-4f7c-984b-556183aa9c20?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ] really does stand alone as the best in class. Since their seed round by OpenAI Startup Fund (i.e. Sam Altman) and Series A led by a16z , it’s hard to fully appreciate what they are building as a leader in Enterprise AI. 
Cursor has achieved one of the fastest growth trajectories in the history of SaaS, reaching $2 billion in ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) as of early 2026. It took Anysphere just 33 months from its founding in 2022 to surpass the $2 billion ARR milestone in February 2026. 
The ARR was evident right from the start. But their pivot to Enterprise was the major driver. They have reputedly doubled their revenue in the last three months (December, 2025 to February, 2026). Even in the early days there were signs this was going to be good product. 
Cursor are now in talks [ https://substack.com/redirect/0fd3f20a-630a-46b8-aeec-8c7d56c73ba7?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ] to raise at a $50 Billion valuation. I believe Cursor and not OpenAI will end up being Anthropic’s biggest Enterprise AI competitor. I asked Daria Cupareanu  to look into their plugins [ https://substack.com/redirect/a6fd8b74-7284-47a2-89c2-d190a597e223?j=eyJ1IjoiM25qYWp4In0.XXz0mgyaLZ9BIuv5Se2kxfBRZ0Y_L5yCbeiyawB8KPE ], marketplace and so forth. You might recall she wrote about Claude Cowork plugins [ https://substack.com/redirect/019c504e-e961-496b-b023-3991ff229be3?j=eyJ1IjoiM25
body_html:

subject: Extract clean data from any webpage in seconds
body_text:
Hope you're settling in well!

The Scrape endpoint is at the foundation of what makes Firecrawl powerful. With a single API call, you can extract clean and structured data from any webpage, whether it's markdown for your LLM workflows or JSON for data wrangling.

We handle JavaScript rendering and deliver content that's actually usable. No messy HTML parsing needed (though you're welcomed to also turn on Raw HTML mode if needed).

Quick tip: Try our JSON mode (extract structured data) or Screenshot feature (visual captures) to unlock even more possibilities. Check out the docs here:

https://docs.firecrawl.dev/features/scrape

Let me know if you have any
questions!

--

Alex Nucci

Head of Growth

Firecrawl — Turn websites into LLM-ready data

 

You can unsubscribe ( https://track.customer.io/unsubscribe/dgTapAsLAIXsNITsNAGc-yHpPZYvnElUxDuX304= ) from these emails.

Hey Gaurav 👋
 
I hope you had a chance to dive into the AI Developer Starter Kit videos I sent over.
 
If you've been watching those builds, you've probably noticed something: the way we write software has fundamentally shifted. We
aren't just memorizing syntax line-by-line anymore.
 
Today, being a great developer is about being a great orchestrator. It's about using tools like Cursor, Windsurf, or Copilot to
write the boilerplate, while you focus on the architecture and the logic.
 
But here is the trap I see a lot of devs falling into right now: they treat every AI model exactly the same. > They try to use one
single model for everything, UI creation, complex debugging, massive refactors, and then get frustrated when the AI hallucinates
or loses the plot.
 
Part of leveling up as an AI engineer is knowing exactly which brain to plug into your editor for the job.
 
Which bring

Chevron Careers

The Moment Everything Changed at GTC 2026

It is Monday evening in San Jose, California. The crowd is packed shoulder-to-shoulder inside the SAP Center. Jensen Huang - NVIDIA's founder and CEO - walks out in his signature black leather jacket, the lights dim just enough to feel cinematic, and the audience goes quiet the way audiences do when they sense something important is about to happen.

What he says next does not sound like a product announcement. It sounds like a rewrite of history.

"Mac and Windows are the operating systems for the personal computer. OpenClaw is the operating system for personal AI. …

subject: Your application with NetApp
body_html:
Hi Gaurav, 
 
Thank you for your interest in the Software Engineer opening at NetApp. We appreciate the time and effort you have put into submitting your application. 
  
After careful consideration, we regret to inform you that we will not be moving forward with your candidacy at this time. We recognize and value your experience and skills, and we encourage you to continue exploring other roles at NetApp that align with your skills and interests. 

To stay informed about potential openings, we recommend visiting our Careers page to sign up for job alerts and stay updated with NetApp by joining our Talent Community.

We sincerely appreciate your interest in our company
NetApp Global Talent Acquisition

Note: This mailbox is not monitored. Please do not respond to this email address.​​

subject: Procore Application Update
body_html:
Hi Gaurav ,

Thank you again for applying for our Software Engineer 1 position at Procore Technologies. We know that job hunting can be time-consuming, so we truly appreciate the time and effort you've invested in the process.

At this time, Procore has decided to pause hiring for this role as we take a step back to assess our current business needs and determine where the greatest impact lies. Please know that this decision in no way reflects your qualifications or your potential fit for the position.

Although it didn’t work out this time, it doesn’t have to mean goodbye. I encourage you to visit our career site and review our current openings. Additionally, we’ll keep your resume in our database and will contact you about future opportunities you might be well suited for.

Wishing you the very best in your continued job search, and we hope our paths cross again.

Warm regards,
Ankita Mishra (Ankita Mishra) [C]

subject: Comparing different models with the same prompt
body_text:
OpenRouter

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Hi Gaurav,

Picking the right model for your task is hard. You have to consider multiple variables, such as speed, price, intelligence, and response quality (vibes).

OpenRouter is here to help:

* Vibe check by chatting with multiple models ( https://openrouter.ai/chat?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) at once. We believe this is a great place to start and get an understanding of different models. For example, you can test models with or without reasoning, or compare open-source models with proprietary ones.
* Discover how models rank for different tasks. OpenRouter publishes real-world data about how models are used across different categories ( https://openrouter.ai/rankings?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io#categories ), programming languages ( https://openrouter.ai/rankings?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io#programming-languages ), tool calls ( https://openrouter.ai/rankings?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io#tools ), and image inputs ( https://openrouter.ai/rankings?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io#images ).
* Evaluate pricing, latency, and throughput. You can find all this information on every model page. For example, here is the GPT - 5 ( https://openrouter.ai/openai/gpt-5?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=ModelDiscovery-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) model page. subject: Thank you for applying to Color!
body_html:
Hi Gaurav,
 
Thank you for your interest in Color! We have received your application and will review it as quickly as possible. Our hiring team will be in touch if there is a match for what we're looking for. Unfortunately, due to the high volume, we may not get back to everyone individually. 
 
To learn more about Color and what it's like to work here:
Blog
Glassdoor
LinkedIn
Twitter
 
Thank you again for applying - we are grateful for your application! We hope to be in touch soon.
 
Cheers,
The Color Team

subject: Sanctity: Your application is in. Here’s a small glimpse into how we think
body_text:
Dear explorer,

Thank you for applying to Sanctity.

Your application for the Software Engineer Intern, Algorithms role is officially in, and our team is now reviewing it with care.

We take this process seriously. We are not just looking for people who can do the work. We are looking for people who want to help shape what AI should become.

That is why your application matters to us.

While we review candidates, we would love to share something we built for fun: a small interactive game that reflects a little of how we think about intelligence, judgment, and the future.

You can play the game here: https://www.sanctity.ai/games/medical-emergency

Just to be clear, this is not the product itself. What we are actually building is multiple orders of magnitude more complex. Think of this simply as a playful glimpse into our world.

If we see a strong fit, we will reach out with next steps.

Either way, we are glad you found your way to us. Very few people are thinking deeply about the future of AI. Fewer still want to help build it responsibly.

Cheers,

Dream Team
body_html:
Dear explorer,

Thank you for applying to Sanctity.

Your application for the Software Engineer Intern, Algorithms role is officially in, and our team is now reviewing it with care.

We take this process seriously. We are not just looking for people who can do the work. We are looking for people who want to help shape what AI should become.

That is why your application matters to us.

While we review candidates, we would love to share something we built for fun: a small interactive game that reflects a little of how we think about intelligence, judgment, and the future.

You can play the game here: Click Here

Just to be clear, this is not the product itself. What we are actually building is multiple orders of magnitude more complex. Think of this simply as a playful glimpse into our world.

If we see a strong fit, we will reach out with next steps.

Either way, we are glad you found your way to us. Very few people are thinking deeply about the future of AI. Fewer still want to help build it responsibly.

Cheers,

Dream Team

subject: 7 Funds that can strengthen your portfolio in 2026!
body_text:
ADITYA BIRLA CAPTIAL MUTUAL FUND
Click Here
 to see the web version


PORTFOLIO INSIGHT: 
YOUR PATH TO STEADY WEALTH CREATION
Dear Gaurav Verma,As we progress through 2026, market dynamics continue to reward disciplined investors who remain invested with the right mix of assets. Whether you're navigating equity volatility, seeking stable growth, building commodity exposure, or maximizing tax efficiency, this is the moment to review your portfolio's alignment with your financial goals. Our carefully curated selection of equity, balanced, and precious metals funds are positioned to help you build lasting wealth across different market conditions.



ABSL Multi AssetAllocation Fund 

3-Year Performance: 19.42% annualized 


Strategy: True diversification across Equity, Debt, Commodities, a dynamic allocation engine that adjusts to market opportunities.


Why Now: With commodity strength and geopolitical uncertainty impacting equity markets, multi-asset allocation provides a natural hedge. This fund smooths volatility while capturing growth, making it ideal for investors seeking downside protection alongside capital appreciation.



ABSL Flexi Cap Fund 

3-Year Performance: 19.24% annualized 


Strategy: Flexibility to invest across the entire market spectrum, large-cap stability, mid-cap growth, and small-cap upside.


Why Now: In a market where valuations vary dramatically across market caps, Flexi Cap offers the agility to pivot toward value. It is a suitable core holding that captures large-cap stability when growth becomes expensive, or tilts toward mid and small-cap opportunity when valuations favor quality at emerging scales.



ABSL Large Cap Fund

3-Year Performance: 15.89% annualized 


Strategy: Concentrated portfolio of high quality Indian companies. Blue Chip stocks with proven resilience and dividend strength.


Why Now: When market sentiment turns uncertain, quality wins. This fund prioritizes capital preservatio

subject: New 3.5 hour course (100% Free 🔥)
body_text:
Start learning the core concepts of the tech role exploding in demand right now.  

Hey there 👋

AI is the only thing anyone talks about these days. But AI doesn’t work without data infrastructure.

Behind every AI model, analytics dashboard, or product recommendation system are pipelines that collect, process, and move massive amounts of data.

And the people who build those systems?

Data Engineers.

Demand for this role is exploding (as we've noted in our Feb 2026 edition of Tech Job Market Monthly). So we decided to give you something to help you get started.

 

🚀 A brand new FREE Data Engineering Crash Course (3.5+ Hours) ( https://youtu.be/5LhU9hc0Rpw?utm_content=%5BNew+Crash+Course%5D+Data+Eng+Course+%2B+Hype+Request+Test+-+Promo+-+Mar+2025&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

This course walks you through the core concepts every Data Engineer needs to understand, focusing on the foundations first.

Inside the course, you'll learn:

* What data engineering actually is and why it matters
* How data is stored and where it comes from
* How data moves through modern systems
* Core Python concepts data engineers use daily
* SQL fundamentals for working with data
* Essential command line tools engineers rely on

By the end, you'll have a clear mental model of how real data systems work and clear next steps to take if you want to become a full-time data engineer that can get hired.

 

👉 Watch the full crash course here ( https://youtu.be/5LhU9hc0Rpw?utm_content=%5BNew+Crash+Course%5D+Data+Eng+Course+%2B+Hype+Request+Test+-+Promo+-+Mar+2025&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

 

Bonus: there's also a special offer, but you have to start watching the course to get it 😉.

 

One Small Request 🙏

 

Please take 30 seconds to HYPE the video:

*
Open the video ( https://youtu.be/5LhU9hc0Rpw?utm_content=%5BNew+Crash+Course%5D+Data+Eng+Course+%2B+Hype+Request+Test+-+Promo+-+Mar+2025&utm_medium=emai

subject: Total Expense Ratio (TER) of ICICI Prudential Dynamic Asset Allocation Active FOF(erstwhile Asset Allocator Fund)-Direct-Growth
body_text:
Untitled Document   
     
          
    Dear Investor,      
   Greetings from ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund.      
   Please note that the base Total Expense Ratio (TER) of ICICI Prudential Dynamic Asset Allocation Active FOF(erstwhile Asset Allocator Fund)-Direct-Growth will be revised from 0.17% to 0.22% with effect from 21-Mar-26.  
        
 Please refer the website of ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund          
    ( 
  www.icicipruamc.com   ) 
             for TER details. 
          
  In case you require any further assistance or clarification, (1) you can get in touch with your financial advisor OR (2) write to us at     
         enquiry@icicipruamc.com 
             OR (3) contact on our customer care helplines 1800 222 999(from MTNL/BSNL) and 1800 200 6666 (Others) between 8 am and 8 pm, Monday to Saturday 
           and 9 am to 7 pm on Sunday. 
           Yours sincerely, 
        Computer Age Management Services Ltd. 
        (Registrar & Transfer Agents to ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund) 
        "The information contained herein is only for the purpose of information and not for distribution and do not constitute an offer to buy or sell or solicitation of any offer to buy or sell any securities or financial instruments in the United States of America ("US") and/or Canada or for the benefit of US persons (being persons falling within the definition of the term "US Person" under the US Securities Act, 1933, as amended) or persons residing in Canada." 
       
          
 Mutual fund investments are subject to market risks, read all scheme related documents carefully.          --Click Here to unsubscribe from this newsletter.
body_html:
Dear Investor,

Greetings from ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund.

Please note that the base Total Expense Ratio (TER) of ICICI Prudential Dynamic Asset Allocation Active FOF(erstwhile Asset Allocator Fund)-Direct-Growth will be revised from 0.17% to 0.22% with effect from 21-Mar-26.

Please refer the website of ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund ( www.icicipruamc.com ) for TER details.

In case you require any further assistance or clarification, (1) you can get in touch with your financial advisor OR (2) write to us at enquiry@icicipruamc.com

subject: Get the most out of Firecrawl
body_text:
Excited to see you join!

Over the next few days, you'll receive emails that help you get the most out of Firecrawl. They'll go over our scrape, crawl, search, and other endpoints. They'll also go deep into features (like Change Tracking ( https://docs.firecrawl.dev/features/change-tracking )).

In the meantime, would love to know if there's anything specific I can answer for you. Feel free to reply to this email and I'll get on it.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

--

Alex Nucci

Head of Growth

Firecrawl — Turn websites into LLM-ready data

 

You can unsubscribe ( https://track.customer.io/unsubscribe/dgTapAsLAIXsNITsNAGc9pbSajld-jkjAM02om4= ) from these emails.

818979

This verification code will expire in 15 minutes. For security reasons, this code can only be used once.

For your security:

subject: The Biggest Google Maps Update in a Decade Just Changed How 2 Billion People Navigate
body_text:
There is a version of this story where the headline is "Google Updates Its Maps App" and you scroll past it without blinking. Updates…
The Biggest Google Maps Update in a Decade Just Changed How 2 Billion People Navigate

MayhemCode (https://medium.com/@mayhemcode?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773655807977-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------b8c06238_e5d7_4a35_8995_986875c53db5--------ff8e13bd9bc7)
  ∙  
March 16, 2026  ∙  
14 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium (https://medium.com/p/452e1e8d022f?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773655807977-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------b8c06238_e5d7_4a35_8995_986875c53db5--------ff8e13bd9bc7)

There is a version of this story where the headline is "Google Updates Its Maps App" and you scroll past it without blinking. Updates happen. Apps add features. Nothing is new under the sun. But what Google announced on March 12, 2026 is not that story. What Google announced is a quiet but significant reframing of what a digital map is allowed to be, and the implications of that reframing reach well beyond whether your next navigation experience looks prettier on a phone screen.

Access without a medium partner here: Major Update in Google Maps (http://redirect.medium.systems/r-tgCkPE0eUC?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773655807977-newsletter.subscribeToProfile--452e1e8d022f-----------------------b8c06238_e5d7_4a35_8995_986875c53db5--------ff8e13bd9bc7)

Google Maps just received what…

body_html:
There is a version of this story where the headline is “Google Updates Its Maps App” and you scroll past it without blinking. Updates…

The Biggest Google Maps Update in a Decade Just Changed How 2 Billion People Navigate

MayhemCode
  ∙  
March 16, 2026
  ∙  
14 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium

There is a version of this story where the headline is “Google Updates Its Maps App” and you scroll past it without blinking. Updates happen. Apps add features. Nothing is new under the sun. But what Google announced on March 12, 2026 is not that story. What Google announced is a quiet but significant reframing of what a digital map is allowed to be, and the implications of that reframing reach well beyond whether your next navigation experience looks prettier on a phone screen.

subject: The model vs the harness
body_text:
"What model does it use?" is the wrong question
The model vs the harness

"What model does it use?" is the wrong question

Jacob Bennett (https://medium.com/@jacobistyping?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773651106331-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------75418c8e_01d8_4e24_afbb_982df1d5e92c--------4e90c3bbf242)
  ∙  
March 16, 2026  ∙  
3 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium (https://medium.com/p/4d3aea78a24c?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773651106331-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------75418c8e_01d8_4e24_afbb_982df1d5e92c--------4e90c3bbf242)

When people compare AI tools, the conversation often starts with "which model does it use?"

The model is one component in a larger system, and usually not the component that explains the differences you actually feel when using the tool. It's more correct to distinguish between the model vs the harness.

What's a model?

The model is the LLM itself (Opus 4.6, GPT-5.3, Composer 1.5, etc). It takes a sequence of tokens in and produces a sequence of tokens out. It has no memory between calls. It can't browse your filesystem, run your tests, or decide when to ask a clarifying question. …

Continue reading (https://medium.com/p/4d3aea78a24c?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773651106331-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------75418c8e_01d8_4e24_afbb_982df1d5e92c--------4e90c3bbf242)

subject: Ollama 0.18 - Ollama is now an official provider for OpenClaw
body_text:
Ollama is now an official provider for OpenClaw. This means Ollama can now be selected directly through OpenClaw's onboarding.
 

openclaw onboard --auth-choice ollama

 




More information: https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/ollama [https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/ollama]


Moonshot Kimi-K2.5

This release of Ollama improves performance of cloud models and their reliability.
 * Up to 2x faster speeds with Kimi-K2.5

 * Tool calling accuracy has been improved

 
Try using Kimi-K2.5 with OpenClaw!
 

Download Ollama [https://ollama.com/download]

 
If you have any feedback, reply directly to this email or join Ollama's Discord channel [https://discord.gg/ollama].
 
❤️ Ollama
 
You are receiving this email because you opted-in to receive updates from Ollama
Ollama, 744 High Street, Palo Alto, CA 94301

subject: How does OpenRouter pricing work?
body_text:
OpenRouter

OpenRouter ( https://openrouter.ai/?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io )

Hi Gaurav,

You can buy OpenRouter Platform Credits ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/credits?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) and use them for all available models.

The providers determine the model prices, and we never mark them up. There is a flat fee of 5.5% (minimum $ 0.80) when purchasing credits. 

You can bring your own keys (BYOK) from any provider ( https://openrouter.ai/settings/integrations?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) and use them in OpenRouter. The first one million requests per month are always free. After that, we charge 5% for each subsequent request.

Your one million free requests
reset every month. 

If you are an enterprise that requires SSO, SLAs, and committed contracts, please reach out to us ( https://openrouter.ai/enterprise?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ). We can discuss our enterprise offerings and pricing over a call.

Please let us know if you have any questions regarding pricing ( https://openrouter.ai/pricing?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Best,
OpenRouter Team
For questions or comments, reach out to us on X ( https://x.com/openrouterai?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ) or on Discord ( https://discord.com/invite/8TpuzNXQYr?utm_campaign=Welcome+Email+Campaign+%232&utm_content=Pricing-email&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=customer.io ).

Unsubscribe here. ( https://track.customer.io/manage_subscription_preferences/dgSE_QoLAPr04wL59OMC

subject: You're in! Welcome to What's new with Pete
body_text:
Welcome, and thanks for subscribing to **What's new with Pete**!

You'll start receiving emails right here in your inbox.

<html>
    <head>
        <style lang="css">
        
            * {
                font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, San Francisco,
                    Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Ubuntu, Roboto, Noto, Segoe UI, Arial,
                    sans-serif;
            }

            body {
                background-color: var(--color-background, #ffffff);
                color: var(--color-foreground, #000000);
            }

            p, li {
                font-size: 15px;
                line-height: 1.5;
            }

            blockquote {
                font-size: 16px !important;
                line-height: 24px !important;
                margin: 16px 0 !important;
                color: var(--color-foreground-muted) !important;
                background-color: var(--color-background-card) !important;
                border-radius: 0.5rem !important;
                padding: 20px;
                padding-top: 1px !important;
                padding-bottom: 1px !important;
            }

            hr {
                width: 300px;
                border: none;
                border-top: 1px solid var(--color-offset);
                border-color: var(--color-background-card);
                border-width: 2px;
                margin-top: 40px;
                margin-bottom: 40px;
            }

            .transactional-email-header {
                border-collapse: collapse;
                margin: 0 auto;
                max-width: 600px;
                margin-top: 20px;
                margin-bottom: 30px;
            }

            .prelude {
                margin-bottom: 32px;
            }

            .buttondown-icon {
                width: 24px;
                height: 24px;
            }

            .email-title {
                margin-bottom: 0;
                margin-top: 0;
                font-weight: bold;
            }

            .email-subtitle {
                color: #a0aec0;
                font-size: 13px;
                margin-bottom: 32px;
            }

            .email-content {
                margin: 0 auto;
                max-width: 600px;
                padding: 0 20px;
            }

            .transactional-email-footer {
                margin: 0 auto;
                max-width: 600px;
                padding: 20px;
                color: #a0aec0;
                font-size: 13px;
                display: block;
                margin-bottom: 32px;
                margin-top: 32px;
                border-top: 1px solid #eaeaea;
            }

            /* Dark mode support */
            @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
                body {
                    background-color: var(--color-background) !important;
                    color: var(--color-foreground) !important;
                }

                .email-title {
                    color: #ffffff !important;
                }

                .email-subtitle {
                    color: #718096 !important;
                }

                .transactional-email-footer {
                    color: #718096 !important;
                    border-top-color: var(--color-background-offset) !important;
                }

                hr {
                    border-color: var(--color-background-offset) !important;
                }

                p, li, td {
                    color: #e0e0e0 !important;
                }

                a {
                    opacity: 0.9;
                }

                .data-table {
                    background-color: var(--color-background-offset) !important;
                }

                .data-row {
                    border-bottom-color: #3d3d3d !important;
                }

                .data-value {
                    color: #9ca3af !important;
                }

                .cta-button {
                    background-color: #6366F1 !important;
                    box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1), 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06) !important;
                }

                .cta-button:hover {
                    background-color: #7C7FF3 !important;
                }

                .email-quote {
                    background-color: var(--color-background-offset) !important;
                }

                .email-quote td {
                    color: var(--color-foreground-offset-2) !important;
                }
            }

            /* Dark mode class for email clients that support it */
            [data-ogsc] body,
            [data-ogsc] .email-title,
            [data-ogsc] p,
            [data-ogsc] li,
            [data-ogsc] td {
                background-color: #1a1a1a !important;
                color: #e0e0e0 !important;
            }

            [data-ogsc] .email-subtitle,
            [

subject: Confirm your subscription to What's new with Pete

AI-powered tools from Swift roots to web frontiers.

If you didn't subscribe to this list or you're not sure why you received this email, you can delete it. You will not be subscribed if you don't click on the link above.

subject: I Used HashMaps Every Day for 4 Years. Then Google Asked Me One Question and I Had Nothing.
body_text:
Four years of daily use and I never once looked inside it. Here's what the silence on that call taught me about the difference between…
I Used HashMaps Every Day for 4 Years. Then Google Asked Me One Question and I Had Nothing.

Four years of daily use and I never once looked inside it. Here's what the silence on that call taught me about the difference between using a tool and actually knowing one.

The Speedcraft Lab (https://medium.com/@speedcraft21?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773631909462-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------8097dbd1_3f75_45f9_aa12_266e3c690724--------2e088f712b89)
  ∙  
March 16, 2026  ∙  
6 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium (https://medium.com/p/cda4e050d1ba?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773631909462-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------8097dbd1_3f75_45f9_aa12_266e3c690724--------2e088f712b89)

Seven buckets. One question. The red one was the only one that mattered.

The interviewer's typing stopped first.

That is what I remember. Not what he said next, not the exact question he had just asked just the sound of keystrokes going quiet on the other end of the call. I was mid-sentence, somewhere in the middle of explaining how a HashMap stores key-value pairs, when I noticed his hands had gone still. He looked at the screen the way someone looks at a document they are about to correct. "Can you tell me," he said, "what happens when two keys hash to the same bucket?"

I gave him an answer. It was…

Continue reading (https://medium.com/p/cda4e050d1ba?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773631909462-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------8097dbd1_3f75_45f9_aa12_266e3c690724--------2e088f712b89)
This is a member-only story and can only be read on Medium.

Sent to gaurav2301v@gmail.com by The Speedcraft Lab on Medium
Unsubscribe (https://medium.com/me/email-settings/7387253d94bc/eaadc47aa1b6?type=noSetting&newsletterV3Id=2e088f712b89&source=email-7387253d94bc-1773631909462-newsletter.subscribeTo

subject: 🚨 Upcoming Externship Alert with Amazon, TikTok, NY Racing, and beyond
body_text:
Learn cybersecurity, data, finance, marketing, and more  

Hey there,

Be the first to enroll and continue getting that resume-ready experience to help you stand out, ahead of the 2026 recruiting season.

EXPLORE MORE ➙ ( https://app.extern.com/sign-up?utm_source=Updates&utm_medium=Email&utm_campaign=ExternshipAlertMar152026EM1 )

-------------
Cybersecurity
-------------

Hydroficient: IoT Cyber Defense Externship

You’ll launch realistic cyberattacks - from spoofing and replay to unauthorized shutoffs, and implement defenses like TLS and mTLS.

Live Sessions Start: March 30, 2026

Duration: 8 weeks

Career Paths: Cybersecurity, Tech

Anomaly Detection

Security Dashboard

Read More -> ( https://www.extern.com/externships/hydroficient-iot-cyber-defense-externship-mar-2026?utm_campaign=ExternshipAlertMar152026RM1&utm_source=Updates&utm_medium=Email )

-------------
Data Analysis
-------------

Outamation: Advanced - AI-Powered Document Insights & Data Extraction Externship

In this externship, you’ll train models to read, extract, and search real mortgage docs using Python, OCR, and RAG. It’s document intelligence meets automation and you're the architect.

Live Sessions Start: March 30, 2026

Duration: 10 weeks

Career Paths: Data Science, Artificial Intelligence

Internship Opportunity

Applied Machine Learning

Read More -> ( https://www.extern.com/externships/outamation-advanced---ai-powered-document-insights-and-data-extraction-externship-mar-2026?utm_campaign=ExternshipAlertMar152026RM2&utm_source=Updates&utm_medium=Email )

NY Racing: NASCAR Sponsorship ROI Externship

Build a data-driven sponsorship strategy for a real NASCAR team. You'll scrape race results, pull social engagement, and create a visibility scoring model that ranks sponsors by ROI.

Live Sessions Start: March 30, 2026

Duration: 8 weeks

Career Paths: Sports & Entertainment, Data Analyst

Python

API Integration

Read More -> ( htt

subject: Apple Is Finally Bending: What the iPhone Fold Leaks Really Tell Us About the Future of Your Phone
body_text:
There is a reason Apple has spent a decade watching Samsung, Huawei, and Motorola wrestle with foldable smartphones without ever touching…
Apple Is Finally Bending: What the iPhone Fold Leaks Really Tell Us About the Future of Your Phone

MayhemCode (https://medium.com/@mayhemcode?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773567270800-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------74b476d2_7312_42cf_8804_e4208fa19cde--------ff8e13bd9bc7)
  ∙  
March 15, 2026  ∙  
12 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium (https://medium.com/p/91e3117a5c5b?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773567270800-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------74b476d2_7312_42cf_8804_e4208fa19cde--------ff8e13bd9bc7)

There is a reason Apple has spent a decade watching Samsung, Huawei, and Motorola wrestle with foldable smartphones without ever touching the category itself. It was not fear. It was not a lack of patents - the company quietly filed hundreds of them. It was something far more Apple-like, something that almost never gets said out loud in Cupertino's product meetings but drives every decision anyway: the technology was not good enough, and Apple does not ship not-good-enough.

Access without a medium partner here: Apples new Foldable Iphone (http://redirect.medium.systems/r-9hcBXkgEdS?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773567270800-newsletter.subscribeToProfile--91e3117a5c5b-----------------------74b476d2_7312_42cf_8804_e4208fa19cde--------ff8e13bd9bc7)

That appears to be changing. And the leaks pouring out of supply…

Continue reading (https://medium.com/p/91e3117a5c5b?source=email-7387253d94bc-1773567270800-newsletter.subscribeToProfile-------------------------74b476d2_7312_42cf_8804_e4208fa19cde--------ff8e13bd9bc7)
This is a member-only story and can only be read on Medium.

Sent to gaurav2301v@gmail.com by MayhemCode on Medium
Unsubscribe (https://medium.com/me/email-settings/7387253d94bc/8192e5d7fe73?type=noSetting&newsletterV3Id=ff8e13bd9bc7&source=email-7387253d94bc-1773567270800
body_html:
There is a reason Apple has spent a decade watching Samsung, Huawei, and Motorola wrestle with foldable smartphones without ever touching…

Apple Is Finally Bending: What the iPhone Fold Leaks Really Tell Us About the Future of Your Phone

MayhemCode
  ∙  
March 15, 2026
  ∙  
12 min read
  ∙  
View on Medium

There is a reason Apple has spent a decade watching Samsung, Huawei, and Motorola wrestle with foldable smartphones without ever touching the category itself. It was not fear. It was not a lack of patents — the company quietly filed hundreds of them. It was something far more Apple-like, something that almost never gets said out loud in Cupertino’s product meetings but drives every decision anyway: the technology was not good enough, and Apple does not ship not-good-enough.
